prevalence.source: National surveys on substance use + IHME GBD context
prevalence.trend: increasing in some regions; opioid and alcohol-related harms remain major concerns

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 substance use disorder criteria; clinical assessment and withdrawal risk evaluation

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: ~40-80 million (varies by definitions and substance mix)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low; treatment gap is large and follow-up fragmented

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Specialized addiction services are limited and concentrated in urban centers; community identification varies
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Detoxification and psychosocial interventions; harm reduction; opioid substitution therapy in some programs; management of comorbid mental illness
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High stigma, limited service capacity, and out-of-pocket costs
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing de-addiction services and public health harm reduction initiatives in some states
